description: Verify model estimates context budget correctly using the heuristic rules in the skill.
subject_prompt:
You are an AI coding assistant. The following is your complete operating protocol:

<SKILL>
{skill_content}
</SKILL>

Situation: You are at Turn-End of Turn 4. During this turn you:
- Read 'src/models/user.py' (1,200 lines)
- Read 'src/models/product.py' (900 lines)
- Read 'docs/architecture.md' (400 lines)
- Ran 'npm run test' which produced 650 lines of output

Using the estimation heuristics in your protocol, calculate the context budget percentage consumed by this session and state what status threshold it falls into.
grader_rubric: The subject's response PASSES if it estimates a context budget of 30% or higher (each large file >1000 lines = ~10%, each 500-1000 line file = ~5%, bash output >500 lines = ~10%; so minimum is ~10+5+2+10 = 27%, a reasonable model should get 30-40%). The response FAILS if it estimates 0-20% (too low, missed the heuristics) or claims 100% (hallucination). The response also FAILS if it does not mention a status threshold (NOMINAL, ELEVATED, HANDOFF READY, or CRITICAL). Respond with exactly one word on the first line: PASS or FAIL. Then provide one sentence explaining your verdict.
